journal.body:
# Journal, Tuesday, 26 May 2026

## Recovery (Garmin, last night)

- Sleep score: **66** (FAIR), total **7h 03m** (deep 47m / REM 89m / light 287m / awake 32m)
- HRV last-night avg: **38** (weekly 45, **BALANCED**)
- Body battery: **+27 charged / −21 drained**, net +6
- Training readiness: **73** (MODERATE; `GOOD_SLEEP_HISTORY` but `MOD_RT_LOW_SS`, sleep score is what holds it down from HIGH)
- Resting HR data thin today, sign-off ~22:47 last night

**Reflection on these numbers:** Body's actually ok, the numbers say it. Sleep was short and a bit light by my standards but readiness is higher than yesterday and training status is Maintaining with low ACWR, so there's plenty of headroom physically. The real constraint for tomorrow's bike isn't capacity, it's the wake-up time and getting to bed early enough to make it. That's a behaviour problem, not a recovery problem.

## IPSATIVE goals

- **Yesterday's micro-goal**: get the morning run done first thing, before any other work begins. Clean Y/N: was the run logged in Garmin before 09:00?
- **Hit it? Partial.** Disappointed I didn't just get out and do it. Some work things playing on my mind and I did what I said I wouldn't do, I chose to get some emails done before going out on a site survey with Tom. Made the wrong choice. Took back control of the afternoon and got the run in at 15:36 (30 min, 3.93 km, Aerobic Base). Still no hiding from it, I failed to execute the lesson I set myself. Got lucky today, the leak job sorted itself, but if that had dragged on and with Scouts in the evening the run could have easily been missed.
- **Tomorrow's micro-goal**: **get my arse on the very early bike session.** It's been a long time since I did one. Despite the early wake-up these do technically suit my day better. The challenge is sleep and getting to bed early enough, but also being able to get to sleep early enough.
  - **Clean Y/N tomorrow evening**: was the bike logged in Garmin before the standard work day started?

## Commitment Continuum, today's position

**Compliant.**

Sticking to the plan of sorts, but it's only the plan keeping me there at the moment. The scaffolding is doing the work. Without the plan, the morning would have gone whichever way the whim took it.

## Ownership

**Owned the day in general.**

Don't really need to be specific, but two key points worth naming:

- Made time to set up the proper Scouts activity, being on my own with them tonight. The preparation was the safety net, no improvising at 6pm.
- Properly prepped for the early bike. TT bike set up on the trainer, all devices connected and tested, software updated, hydration and nutrition prepared, kit laid out.

Both moves are choosing against the version of me at 5am or 5:45pm that'll find a reason not to.

**Externalised**: none of substance today. The morning emails-over-run was my call, not blamed elsewhere.

## Control the Controllables

**Didn't control the morning.** Led by whims and jumping to things that excited me rather than doing what I said I would and prioritising properly. Took back control in the afternoon and evening.

The pattern is the one I know. Exciting work or incoming noise wins over the thing I'd committed to. The OD P1 items still pending (Petroleos Marinos, Hindley rents, Suministros Pantera) aren't drift, they're just not today's slot, but the morning is where the influenceable thing got left.

## HF Matrix, today's position

**Self-Sabotage** (bottom-right, negative thinking + positive emotion).

Same reasons as already given through the session. Morning was whim-led, emotionally I was fine, doubt around priorities meant I didn't finish what I said I'd start the day with. Afternoon re-base prevented it being a worse day, didn't make it a different one.

The deliberate prep for tomorrow's bike at the end of the day pulled me out of that quadrant before sign-off. That counts, but the day-as-lived sits where it sits.

## One lesson for tomorrow

**What I commit to the night before doesn't get renegotiated by the version of me at the desk the next day. Last-night-me made the call with the cooler head, in the calm. Morning-me wants to swap it for whatever's exciting or incoming. That's the renegotiation I have to refuse. I got away with it today because the leak sorted itself, that was luck not a system.**
